chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-641 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For order on MA No.2663/2026. 2. For order on office objection. 3. For order on MA No.2664/2026. 4. For order on MA No.2665/2026. 5. For hearing of main case. 14.04.2026 Mr. Rashid Raees @ B.M. advocate for petitioner. 1. Urgency granted. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 2,4&5. Petitioner prays for quashing the following FIRs: (i) FIR No.261/2025 under sections 147, 149, 504, 337-A (i), F (i) PPC; and (ii) FIR No.66/2026 under sections 447, 511, 452, 147, 149, 504, 506, 337-A (i), F (i) PPC. Both FIRs registered at PS B-Section Latifabad Hyderabad. As regards FIR No.261/2025, admittedly a challan has been filed before the concerned Magistrate. As regards FIR No.66/2026, learned counsel submits that the same is still under investigation. Regards FIR No.261/2025, learned counsel is queried whether the petitioner availed the alternate remedy available before the Magistrate for premature acquittal under section 249- A Cr.P.C. He concedes that the petitioner did not. The petition does not bring forth any exceptional circumstances to by-pass said remedy in the exercise of constitutional jurisdiction. With regard to FIR No.66/2026, learned counsel has not been able to convince us to intervene in the on-going investigation of the case. Suffice to say that once investigation is complete, the petitioner will have a remedy before the trial Court under section 249-A or 265-K Cr.P.C., as the case may be. For the foregoing reasons, the petition is dismissed along with listed applications.

Suffice to say that once investigation is complete, the petitioner will have a remedy before the trial Court under section 249-A or 265-K Cr.P.C., as the case may be. For the foregoing reasons, the petition is dismissed along with listed applications. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
